blocks.spoken: Well, 1114 has uh one of the mother of all TVs associated with it. It is error it's poor error handling in in the MTLS server. Essentially what happens is a web socket connection is instantiated, that is bundled in a TLS pipe, if you like, so the encryption is established and then the web socket communication goes over that. There is incorrect handling of the web socket error code, so the tunnel would stay open and we could then send whatever commands we wanted through. Upgrade: websocket
Connection: upgrade

" else request = "GET #{metrics} HTTP/1.1

" end puts request ssl_socket.puts request puts ssl_socket.gets else # NOT EXPLOITING. Further requests will fail" end

segment_summary: This segment focuses on security testing, specifically demonstrating how to find and exploit insecure Kubernetes clusters. The speaker introduces BinaryEdge as a tool for discovering open Kubernetes API endpoints on the internet. A live demo showcases an Nmap script used to identify version information leakage from a Kubernetes API server. The speaker then explains a critical vulnerability (CVE) related to poor error handling in the MTLS server, which allows an unauthenticated client to exploit a malicious websocket upgrade, potentially leading to privilege escalation. The segment concludes with lessons on avoiding public API server endpoints and the importance of layered security.
